title: Kentucky Fried Chicken resumes normal operations after resolving cyberattack on Nichirei
summary: The fast-food chain recovers its delivery service and reopens all its stores after the ransomware attack on logistics company Nichirei disrupted operations.

title: Fire in a home in Yachiyo, Chiba, leaves four dead
summary: Authorities found the lifeless bodies of four people among the rubble of a completely burned two-story house in the city of Yachiyo.

title: Yen falls to 39-year lows, surpassing 163 yen per dollar
summary: The Japanese currency weakens to levels not seen since 1985, driven by dollar buying and geopolitical uncertainty, while the government hints at possible intervention.

title: Ukraine dismisses its military commander amid protests and tensions with Defense Minister
summary: President Zelensky removes General Syrskyi after the dismissal of the Defense Minister, causing citizen protests and criticism over instability in military leadership.

title: Trump threatens to attack Iran's underground nuclear facilities and war costs exceed 6 trillion yen
summary: The US president announces an imminent attack on Mount Pickax in Iran, while the Pentagon estimates that military operation costs exceed 6 trillion yen (about 40 billion dollars).

title: Japan records trade deficit for second consecutive month due to high cost of oil imports
summary: The energy bill skyrockets due to volatility in the Middle East, raising the unit price of imported crude to historic highs, with a 560% increase in purchases from the United States.

title: Japanese government revises 'Basic Policy' with emphasis on defense and BOJ independence, removing mention of Tokyo concentration
summary: The new draft economic policy reflects Prime Minister Takaichi's mark, prioritizing reform of the armed forces in five years and fiscal discipline without setting numerical targets for military spending.

title: Extreme heatwave in Japan: first temperatures of 40 °C in Gifu and Aichi and at least four dead from heatstroke
summary: Authorities issue warnings about dangerous heat in the Kanto and Tokai regions, as the first heat-related deaths of the year are recorded and sudden storms are expected in the afternoon in Tohoku.

title: France approves ban on social media use for children under 15, the first in Europe
summary: The law will come into force in September and seeks to protect adolescents from the dangers of digital platforms, making France the first European country to impose such a restriction.

title: First arrest in Japan of a woman for alleged involvement in illegal fund transfer crimes
summary: The 40-year-old suspect from Okinawa is accused of acting as a 'collector' in a money laundering network through fraudulent part-time jobs, in what is the first arrest for this type of crime.

title: Kyoto's Gion festival displays its traditional float decorated with pine panels and begins with the first parade
summary: The emblematic Japanese festival presents its famous floats and celebrations, including the exhibition of panels painted with nature motifs and the inaugural parade ceremony.

title: Wreck of Japanese cruiser 'Nagara' sunk in WWII located and filmed for the first time
summary: An underwater expedition manages to capture images of the warship, which lies off the coast of Kumamoto, where 348 crew members lost their lives in 1944.

title: Japanese and Wall Street stock markets rally driven by rise in technology and semiconductor stocks
summary: The Nikkei and Dow Jones record strong gains, with the Japanese index exceeding 67,000 points, thanks to optimism generated by good quarterly results from chip sector companies.

title: 'Subsidiary capital' bill enters debate in the Diet with tight schedule and critical opposition
summary: The Senate special commission begins discussion of the controversial initiative that seeks to create a second capital, while the government rushes to approve it before the end of the legislature.
